skill.name: priority-judge
skill.description: Helps users sort messy task lists by judging clarity, deadlines, and the next one or two priorities to act on.
skill.summary: A conversational priority assistant for choosing what to do now.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks, weak cryptography, and high entropy, but manual review found only human-readable Markdown guidance. The backtick alerts are Markdown code fences and inline examples, with no executable scripts, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempt found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The seven external_commands alerts point to Markdown code fences and inline filename examples used to explain the priority workflow. No shell command, Ruby execution, user-controlled command construction, or executable script behavior is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The marked locations are visible Markdown examples and prose, not code that a runtime would execute. The file contains no script wrapper or command invocation context around those backticks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Heuristic
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm alert maps to the frontmatter description line. Manual review found no cryptographic API, hash algorithm, encryption routine, or password handling behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a natural-language Chinese description of the skill purpose. There is no semantic evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or any other weak cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy on Readable Markdown
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The entropy heuristic appears to be triggered by multilingual Markdown content, not packed, encrypted, or binary data. The file is readable documentation for a task prioritization workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed a normal Markdown document with Chinese text and headings. No encoded payload, hidden binary content, or obfuscation pattern was found.

content.value_statement: Busy users often have many tasks but no clear order. This skill turns a messy list into a focused decision based on clarity, deadlines, and immediate action.
actual_capabilities: Collects unsorted tasks through a guided conversation.
Asks whether each task is clear, urgent, and specific.
Classifies tasks by clarity and deadline status.
Recommends the top one or two tasks to do first.
Can format the priority result as a Markdown planning note.
limitations: Requires the user to provide accurate task and deadline details.
Does not connect to calendars, email, or project management tools.
Does not estimate effort with formal project management methods.
Provides productivity guidance, not medical, legal, or financial advice.

use_cases.title: Choose Today’s Focus
use_cases.description: Turn a long personal task list into one or two actions for the current day.

use_cases.title: Sort Creative Work
use_cases.description: Separate clear creative tasks from vague ideas that need more thinking first.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Weekly Priority Note
use_cases.description: Review deadlines and create a simple Markdown note for the week.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Priority Review
prompt_templates.prompt: I have many things to do. Please help me sort them and choose what I should do first.

prompt_templates.title: Sort by Deadline and Clarity
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my task list. Ask me about clarity and deadlines, then rank the tasks by what needs action today.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Focused Work Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose one or two tasks for a focused work session. Ignore tasks that are unclear or have no deadline.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Weekly Priority Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Interview me about my current tasks, classify each task, and produce a concise Markdown priority note for this week.

output_examples.input: I need to write a report, reply to messages, explore a product idea, and prepare slides by tomorrow.
output_examples.output: Top priority: prepare the slides, because they have a deadline tomorrow. Second priority: write the report if its deadline is near. Product idea exploration should wait until the urgent work is clear.

output_examples.input: I have many creative ideas, but only two are clear. None have a deadline.
output_examples.output: Start with one clear idea if you have spare energy. Do not force unclear ideas now. Put unclear ideas into a later thinking list.

output_examples.input: I want a weekly priority note for tasks with deadlines, unclear tasks, and optional work.
output: Must do this week: tasks with clear next steps and deadlines.
Clarify first: deadline tasks that still need decisions.

best_practices: List all tasks first, even if they feel messy or incomplete.
Give real deadline information instead of guessing urgency.
Use the final result to start one task immediately.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a full project management system.
Do not hide important deadlines or dependencies.
Do not ask it to optimize tasks that are still undefined.

faq.question: What does this skill help me decide?
faq.answer: It helps you decide what to do now when many tasks compete for attention.

faq.question: How does it rank tasks?
faq.answer: It compares whether each task is clear and whether it has a deadline.

faq.question: Can it create a written plan?
faq.answer: Yes. It can format the priority list as a Markdown note when requested.

faq.question: Does it manage calendar events?
faq.answer: No. It relies on the task and deadline information that you provide.

faq.question: Is it useful for creative work?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed for work where some tasks are clear and others need more thinking.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
